name: Gitea API
what_it_does: Provides a full REST API for a self-hosted Git service, enabling agents to manage repositories, issues, pull requests, users, organizations, releases, and webhooks on a Gitea instance.
use_cases: Automatically create and configure repositories with branch protection rules and webhooks for new projects
Open, comment on, and merge pull requests as part of an automated code review or release pipeline
Query open issues and assign them to team members based on label or milestone criteria
Create releases and upload build artifacts to a Gitea instance from a CI/CD automation agent
Mirror repositories from external sources into a self-hosted Gitea org for compliance archiving
not_for: Instances you do not self-host — Gitea has no managed cloud offering, so you must operate the server
Real-time streaming of git push events — webhooks are outbound only with no inbound streaming API
Large-scale hosted SaaS with guaranteed uptime — reliability depends entirely on your own infrastructure
best_when: You control a self-hosted Gitea instance and need full programmatic access to all forge features without vendor lock-in or usage-based pricing.
avoid_when: You need a managed hosted Git service with SLA guarantees, as Gitea requires you to operate and maintain the server yourself.

auth.notes: Access tokens support fine-grained scopes (repo, issue, admin, package, etc.). OAuth2 app flow available. Basic auth deprecated in newer versions. For agents, scoped access tokens are recommended.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Fully free and open-source; no usage limits imposed by Gitea itself
pricing.paid_tiers: No paid tiers from Gitea; hosting costs are your own infrastructure
pricing.notes: Free and self-hosted. Gitea Cloud (managed) has been announced but is not widely available as of this evaluation.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limiting is configurable by the instance operator; default is generous (often unlimited) for self-hosted deployments

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API returns standard HTTP status codes with JSON error bodies. Error messages are generally descriptive. Swagger UI is available at /api/swagger on any Gitea instance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Repository and user creation are not idempotent — creating an existing resource returns a 409 Conflict. Agents should check existence first. Label and milestone creation have similar behavior.
known_agent_gotchas: Pagination uses page/limit query params, not cursors — agents iterating large repos/issues must handle off-by-one on total count
Admin-level API endpoints require the token owner to have site admin privileges; permission errors are returned as 403 with minimal detail
Webhook delivery is best-effort with no built-in retry tracking via API — agents relying on webhooks should implement their own idempotency
The API version (v1) has been stable but some endpoints were added in newer Gitea releases — behavior varies by instance version
Token creation via API requires an existing session or admin token; bootstrapping access for a new instance requires manual first-token creation

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS is optional and operator-configured (not enforced by default). Scoped tokens with fine-grained permissions are well-implemented. Self-hosted nature means security posture depends heavily on operator configuration.
